title: Storage Path — Cluster Failover or Multipath Events

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF PR.IR-02 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF PR.IR-02 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.30: Storage Path — Cluster Failover or Multipath Events.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.30' running on vmware:inv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=infra sourcetype=vmware:inv earliest=-7d failover OR "path down"
| stats count by host, Message
description: Surfaces storage or hypervisor failover events evidencing infrastructure resilience monitoring.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes infra and sourcetypes vmware:inv. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Storage Path — Cluster Failover or Multipath Events. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Normalize messages per vendor; (2) alert spikes; (3) tie to capacity planning. Indexes required: `index=infra`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vmware:inv`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: VMware or SAN logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:inv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize messages per vendor; (2) alert spikes; (3) tie to capacity planning.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=infra sourcetype=vmware:inv earliest=-7d failover OR "path down"
| stats count by host, Message
```

Understanding this SPL

**Storage Path — Cluster Failover or Multipath Events (PR.IR-02)** — Surfaces storage or hypervisor failover events evidencing infrastructure resilience monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:inv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): VMware or SAN logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: infra; **sourcetype**: vmware:inv. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=infra, sourcetype=vmware:inv, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, Message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar chart
knownFalsePositives: Protect-control failures during planned exception windows, application compatibility testing, or vendor lag.
